pressure.definition: The force that gas particles apply to the walls of their container, divided by the area of those walls. Measured in pascals (Pa) or kilopascals (kPa).

ideal-gas.definition: A theoretical gas whose particles have no volume of their own and don't attract each other. Real gases approximate this at low pressure and high temperature.

van-der-waals.definition: An equation that improves on PV = nRT for real gases by adding two correction terms: one for the volume of the particles themselves, and one for the attractive forces between them.

kinetic-energy.definition: The energy a particle has due to its motion. Higher temperature means higher average kinetic energy.
